README.html.title: Invex.StructuredText | Invex.StructuredText
README.html.summary: Invex.StructuredText A strongly-typed C# library for generating structured YAML text files, specifically GitHub Actions workflows, Dependabot configurations, and Azure DevOps Pipelines, using a fluent, type-safe API with a powerful expression system. Features Type-safe workflow authoring Define CI/CD pipelines as C# objects with full IntelliSense and compile-time validation Unified expression system Build complex expressions (conditions, string interpolation, functions) that compile to platform-specific syntax GitHub Actions Full support for workflow YAML generation including triggers, jobs, steps, matrix strategies, permissions, concurrency, containers, and more Dependabot Generate dependabot.yml configurations with registries, update rules, groups, and schedules Azure DevOps Pipelines Comprehensive pipeline generation with stages, jobs, deployment strategies (runOnce, rolling, canary), resources, templates, and parameters Packages Package Description Invex.StructuredText Core library with StructuredTextWriter and the expression system Invex.StructuredText.GithubActions GitHub Actions workflow and Dependabot config generation Invex.StructuredText.AzureDevopsPipelines Azure DevOps Pipelines YAML generation Installation dotnet add package Invex.StructuredText.GithubActions # or dotnet add package Invex.StructuredText.AzureDevopsPipelines Both platform packages depend on Invex.StructuredText core, so it will be installed automatically. Documentation Comprehensive guides live in the docs/ directory: Introduction — what this library is and why it exists Getting Started — install and generate your first file Expressions — the unified expression system GitHub Actions — workflow generation in depth Dependabot — dependabot.yml generation Azure DevOps Pipelines — pipelines, stages, jobs, deployment strategies Architecture — how the pieces fit together FAQ An auto-generated API reference is built with docfx from the XML documentation comments. Quick Start GitHub Actions var workflow = new GithubAction { Name = "CI", On = [ new On.Push { Branches = ["main"], BranchesIgnore = null, Tags = null, TagsIgnore = null, Paths = null, PathsIgnore = null, }, ], Jobs = [ new Job { Name = new RawExpression("build"), RunsOn = new() { Labels = [new RawExpression("ubuntu-latest")] }, Steps = [ new Step.UsesStep { Name = new RawExpression("Checkout"), Uses = new RawExpression("actions/checkout@v4"), }, new Step.RunStep { Name = new RawExpression("Build"), Run = ["dotnet build --configuration Release"], }, ], }, ], }; var writer = new GithubActionWriter(); writer.Write(workflow); var yaml = writer.TextWriter.ToString(); Azure DevOps Pipelines var pipeline = new DevopsPipeline.DevopsPipelineWithSteps { Trigger = new Trigger.BranchList { Branches = ["main"] }, Steps = [ new Step.Script { ScriptContent = new RawExpression("echo Hello, world!") }, ], }; var writer = new DevopsPipelineWriter(); writer.Write(pipeline); var yaml = writer.TextWriter.ToString(); Expressions The expression system provides a fluent API for building platform-specific expressions that are formatted differently depending on the target (GitHub Actions uses ${{ }} syntax, Azure DevOps uses its own macro/runtime expression syntax). // Reference step outputs var output = new StepOutputExpression { StepName = "build-step", OutputName = "artifact-path", }; // Use conditions var condition = output.Contains("release").Evaluate(); // String interpolation with format expressions var formatted = TextExpressions.Format($"Release-{output}"); // Logic operators var combined = output.Contains("main") & new BooleanExpression(true); Architecture Core (Invex.StructuredText) StructuredTextWriter: A low-level indentation-aware text writer that handles YAML-style output with automatic indent management via IDisposable scopes Expression System: A rich set of record types (TextExpression hierarchy) representing values, functions, logic operators, and workflow-specific constructs (step outputs, job outcomes, etc.) TextExpressionFormatter: Base class for platform-specific expression formatters GitHub Actions (Invex.StructuredText.GithubActions) GithubActionWriter: Serializes a GithubAction model to valid workflow YAML DependabotConfigWriter: Serializes a DependabotConfig model to valid dependabot.yml GithubExpressionFormatter: Formats expressions using GitHub's ${{ }} syntax Model types: Strongly-typed records for all GitHub Actions concepts (triggers, jobs, steps, matrix, permissions, etc.) Azure DevOps Pipelines (Invex.StructuredText.AzureDevopsPipelines) DevopsPipelineWriter: Serializes a DevopsPipeline model to valid pipeline YAML DevopsExpressionFormatter: Formats expressions using Azure DevOps syntax Model types: Strongly-typed records/unions for pipelines, stages, jobs, steps, resources, triggers, variables, deployment strategies, and more Requirements .NET 8.0, .NET 9.0, or .NET 10.0 License This project is licensed under the MIT License.

api/Invex.StructuredText.AzureDevopsPipe.title: Class DevopsExpressionFormatter | Invex.StructuredText
api/Invex.StructuredText.AzureDevopsPipe.summary: Class DevopsExpressionFormatter Namespace Invex.StructuredText.AzureDevopsPipelines Assembly Invex.StructuredText.AzureDevopsPipelines.dll Renders TextExpression trees using Azure DevOps expression syntax: logic and comparison operators render as functions (and(...), or(...), not(...), eq(...), ne(...), lt(...), …), EvaluateExpression becomes a compile-time template expression ${{ ... }}, DevopsRuntimeExpression becomes a runtime expression $[ ... ], and DevopsMacroExpression becomes a macro expansion $(variable). Step and job references render against the steps. and dependencies. contexts. [PublicAPI] public sealed class DevopsExpressionFormatter : TextExpressionFormatter, ITextExpressionFormatter Inheritance object TextExpressionFormatter DevopsExpressionFormatter Implements ITextExpressionFormatter Inherited Members TextExpressionFormatter.Format(TextExpression) object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods Resolve(TextExpression) Rewrites a single expression node into its Azure DevOps textual form. protected override TextExpression? Resolve(TextExpression expression) Parameters expression TextExpression The node to rewrite. Returns TextExpression Exceptions ArgumentOutOfRangeException Thrown for unsupported expression types.

api/Invex.StructuredText.AzureDevopsPipe.title: Namespace Invex.StructuredText.AzureDevopsPipelines.DevopsPipelineModel.Steps | Invex.StructuredText
api/Invex.StructuredText.AzureDevopsPipe.summary: Namespace Invex.StructuredText.AzureDevopsPipelines.DevopsPipelineModel.Steps Classes SettableVariables Restrictions on which variables can be set from a step. Step Steps are a linear sequence of operations that make up a job. Step.Bash Runs a script in Bash on Windows, macOS, and Linux. Step.Checkout Configure how the pipeline checks out source code. Step.Download Downloads artifacts associated with the current run or from another Azure Pipeline. Step.DownloadBuild Downloads build artifacts. Step.GetPackage Downloads a package from a package management feed in Azure Artifacts or Azure DevOps Server. Step.PowerShell Runs a script using either Windows PowerShell (on Windows) or pwsh (Linux and macOS). Step.Publish Publishes (uploads) a file or folder as a pipeline artifact. Step.Pwsh Runs a script in PowerShell Core on Windows, macOS, and Linux. Step.ReviewApp Creates a resource dynamically under a deploy phase provider. Step.Script Runs a script using cmd.exe on Windows and Bash on other platforms. Step.Task Runs a task. Step.Template Reference to a step template. Step0MatchExtensions StepTarget Environment in which to run a step. StepTarget.TargetName Simple target name. StepTarget.TargetSpec Full target specification. StepTarget0MatchExtensions

api/Invex.StructuredText.AzureDevopsPipe.summary: Namespace Invex.StructuredText.AzureDevopsPipelines Classes DevopsExpressionFormatter Renders TextExpression trees using Azure DevOps expression syntax: logic and comparison operators render as functions (and(...), or(...), not(...), eq(...), ne(...), lt(...), …), EvaluateExpression becomes a compile-time template expression ${{ ... }}, DevopsRuntimeExpression becomes a runtime expression $[ ... ], and DevopsMacroExpression becomes a macro expansion $(variable). Step and job references render against the steps. and dependencies. contexts. DevopsMacroExpression Wraps a variable name to be expanded using Azure DevOps macro syntax: $(variableName) DevopsPipelineWriter Serializes a DevopsPipeline model to Azure Pipelines YAML. Expressions embedded in the model are rendered with DevopsExpressionFormatter. DevopsRuntimeExpression Wraps an expression to be evaluated at runtime using Azure DevOps runtime expression syntax: $[ expression ]

api/Invex.StructuredText.Expressions.Tex.summary: Class TextExpressionFormatter Namespace Invex.StructuredText.Expressions Assembly Invex.StructuredText.dll Base class for platform-specific expression formatters. [PublicAPI] public abstract class TextExpressionFormatter : ITextExpressionFormatter Inheritance object TextExpressionFormatter Implements ITextExpressionFormatter Derived DevopsExpressionFormatter GithubExpressionFormatter Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Format(TextExpression?) is implemented as a rewrite loop: RawExpression yields its text, ConcatExpression concatenates its formatted parts, and CastExpression<TTo> is unwrapped transparently. Every other node is passed to Resolve(TextExpression), which must rewrite it into a simpler expression; the loop repeats until everything bottoms out in raw text. Methods Format(TextExpression?) Renders expression to platform-specific text, or returns null when the expression is null. public string? Format(TextExpression? expression) Parameters expression TextExpression The expression tree to render. Returns string Exceptions InvalidOperationException Thrown when Resolve(TextExpression) cannot handle an expression node. Resolve(TextExpression) Rewrites expression into a simpler expression — typically a RawExpression containing the platform-specific rendering of the node. Implementations may call Format(TextExpression?) recursively to render sub-expressions. protected abstract TextExpression? Resolve(TextExpression expression) Parameters expression TextExpression The node to rewrite. Never a raw, concat, or cast expression. Returns TextExpression The rewritten expression, or null if the node is not supported.

api/Invex.StructuredText.Expressions.htm.title: Namespace Invex.StructuredText.Expressions | Invex.StructuredText
api/Invex.StructuredText.Expressions.htm.summary: Namespace Invex.StructuredText.Expressions Classes AndExpression Logical AND over Source. The platform formatter decides the rendered syntax (e.g. a && b or and(a, b)). BooleanExpression A boolean literal, rendered as true or false. CastExpression<TTo> Wraps an expression to reinterpret its result type at compile time. The formatter should treat this transparently — it simply delegates to the inner expression. CoalesceExpression Returns the first non-empty value of Source, rendered as coalesce(...). ConcatExpression Concatenates the rendered text of Values with no separator. Handled directly by TextExpressionFormatter, so it works on every platform. ContainsExpression Tests whether Source contains Pattern, rendered as contains(source, pattern). EndsWithExpression Tests whether Source ends with Pattern, rendered as endsWith(source, pattern). EqualExpression Equality comparison. The platform formatter decides the rendered syntax (e.g. left == right or eq(left, right)). EvaluateExpression Marks Expression for evaluation in the target platform's expression context. FormatExpression Replaces placeholders ({0}, {1}, …) in Source with Arguments, rendered as format(source, args...). Usually built via TextExpressions.Format($"...") string interpolation. GreaterThanExpression Greater-than comparison. The platform formatter decides the rendered syntax (e.g. left > right or gt(left, right)). GreaterThanOrEqualToExpression Greater-than-or-equal comparison. The platform formatter decides the rendered syntax (e.g. left >= right or ge(left, right)). HashFilesExpression Returns a hash of the files matched by the glob pattern(s) in Source, rendered as hashFiles(source). Commonly used for cache keys. IndexAccessExpression Accesses an element of Array by Index, rendered as array[index]. JoinExpression Joins the elements of the array-valued Source with OptionalSeparator (comma when omitted), rendered as join(source, separator). LessThanExpression Less-than comparison. The platform formatter decides the rendered syntax (e.g. left < right or lt(left, right)). LessThanOrEqualToExpression Less-than-or-equal comparison. The platform formatter decides the rendered syntax (e.g. left <= right or le(left, right)). NotEqualExpression Inequality comparison. The platform formatter decides the rendered syntax (e.g. left != right or ne(left, right)). NotExpression Logical negation of Source. The platform formatter decides the rendered syntax (e.g. prefix ! or not(source)). NullExpression The null literal, rendered as empty text. NumberExpression A numeric literal, rendered using the invariant culture. OrExpression Logical OR over Source. The platform formatter decides the rendered syntax (e.g. a || b or or(a, b)). PropertyAccessExpression Accesses Property of Object, rendered as object.property. RawExpression Verbatim, uninterpreted text. The formatter writes Value exactly as-is. Use this for plain YAML values and context path references; use StringExpression for quoted string literals inside expressions. StartsWithExpression Tests whether Source starts with Pattern, rendered as startsWith(source, pattern). StepOutcomeExpression References the outcome of a step in the current job, rendered as steps.<step>.outcome. Compare against a StepOutcomeTypeExpression. StepOutcomeTypeExpression A step outcome literal for comparison with StepOutcomeExpression. The platform formatter renders the appropriate outcome string for the target platform. StepOutputExpression References an output of a step in the current job. The platform formatter renders the appropriate context path for the target platform. StringExpression A string literal inside an expression, rendered with quotes (e.g. 'value'). Use RawExpression for verbatim, unquoted text. TargetOutcomeExpression References the outcome (result/status) of another job or stage. The platform formatter renders the appropriate context path for the target platform. Compare against a TargetOutcomeTypeExpression. TargetOutcomeTypeExpression A job/stage outcome literal for comparison with TargetOutcomeExpression. The platform formatter renders the appropriate outcome string for the target platform. TargetOutputExpression References an output of another job or stage. The platform formatter renders the appropriate context path for the target platform. TextExpression The base type of the platform-agnostic expression tree. Expressions are immutable records composed via fluent methods and operators, then rendered to platform-specific text by a TextExpressionFormatter. TextExpressionCollection An ordered collection of TextExpression instances. Implicit conversions from string, string[], List<T>, and single expressions allow model properties to be assigned naturally, e.g. Run = ["dotnet build"] or Branches = ["main"]. TextExpressionFormatter Base class for platform-specific expression formatters. TextExpressionUtils Utility helpers for working with sequences and trees of TextExpression. TextExpressions The entry point for creating TextExpression instances. Factory members (Raw, From, Format, Concat, True, False, Null, …) are attached to this class via extension members in WorkflowExpressionExtensions. ToJsonExpression Converts Source to its JSON representation, rendered as the platform's JSON serialization function. WorkflowExpressionCollection<T> A typed workflow expression collection that documents the expected element result type. The type parameter serves as a compile-time hint for what each expression resolves to. WorkflowExpressionExtensions Extension members that augment TextExpressions and TextExpression with factory helpers for building expression trees. Structs TextExpressionInterpolatedStringHandler An interpolated string handler that creates a FormatExpression from an interpolated string. Literal parts are combined into a format string with placeholders, and TextExpression holes become arguments. WorkflowExpression<T> A typed workflow expression that documents the expected result type of the expression. The type parameter serves as a compile-time hint for what the expression resolves to. Interfaces ITextExpressionFormatter Renders a TextExpression tree into platform-specific text. ITextExpression<TResult> Marker interface that documents the result type an expression is expected to resolve to. The type parameter is a compile-time hint only; it has no effect on formatting. Enums StepOutcomeTypeExpression.OutcomeType The possible outcomes of a step. TargetOutcomeTypeExpression.OutcomeType The possible outcomes of a job or stage.

api/Invex.StructuredText.GithubActions.D.summary: Enum VersioningStrategy Namespace Invex.StructuredText.GithubActions.DependabotConfigModel.Model Assembly Invex.StructuredText.GithubActions.dll How to update manifest version requirements. [PublicAPI] public enum VersioningStrategy Fields Auto = 0 Try to differentiate between apps and libraries. Use 'increase' for apps and 'widen' for libraries. Increase = 1 Always increase the minimum version requirement to match the new version. IncreaseIfNecessary = 2 Leave the constraint if the original constraint allows the new version, otherwise, bump the constraint. LockfileOnly = 3 Only create pull requests to update lockfiles. Ignore any new versions that would require package manifest changes. Widen = 4 Widen the allowed version requirements to include both the new and old versions, when possible.

api/Invex.StructuredText.GithubActions.G.title: Class On.Push | Invex.StructuredText
api/Invex.StructuredText.GithubActions.G.summary: Class On.Push Namespace Invex.StructuredText.GithubActions.GithubActionModel Assembly Invex.StructuredText.GithubActions.dll Triggers when commits or tags are pushed (push), with optional branch/tag/path filters. All filter properties are required so that omissions are explicit; pass null for filters you do not want. api/Invex.StructuredText.GithubActions.G.title: Class On | Invex.StructuredText
api/Invex.StructuredText.GithubActions.G.summary: Class On Namespace Invex.StructuredText.GithubActions.GithubActionModel Assembly Invex.StructuredText.GithubActions.dll A workflow trigger event (on). This is a discriminated union over the GitHub webhook events; construct the variant for the event you want, e.g. new On.Push { ... } or new On.Schedule(["0 6 * * 1"]). Enum member names intentionally match the lowercase/snake_case activity-type strings used in workflow YAML. api/Invex.StructuredText.GithubActions.G.title: Namespace Invex.StructuredText.GithubActions.GithubActionModel | Invex.StructuredText
api/Invex.StructuredText.GithubActions.G.summary: Namespace Invex.StructuredText.GithubActions.GithubActionModel Classes Concurrency Concurrency settings (concurrency) that ensure only one run (or queued run) sharing the same group executes at a time, at either workflow or job level. Container A Docker container that a job runs in (container) or that hosts a service (services). Credentials Credentials for a private container registry (credentials), passed to docker login. Typically reference secrets, e.g. ${{ secrets.REGISTRY_PASSWORD }}. Environment The deployment environment a job targets (environment). Environments can carry protection rules and secrets configured in the repository settings. GithubAction The root model of a GitHub Actions workflow file (.github/workflows/*.yml). Serialize it with GithubActionWriter. Job A job within a workflow: a set of steps that execute on the same runner. Matrix A matrix of variable combinations to run a job with (strategy.matrix). On A workflow trigger event (on). This is a discriminated union over the GitHub webhook events; construct the variant for the event you want, e.g. new On.Push { ... } or new On.Schedule(["0 6 * * 1"]). Enum member names intentionally match the lowercase/snake_case activity-type strings used in workflow YAML. On.BranchProtectionRule Triggers when a branch protection rule changes (branch_protection_rule). On.CheckRun Triggers on check run activity (check_run). On.CheckSuite Triggers on check suite activity (check_suite). On.Create Triggers when a branch or tag is created (create). On.Delete Triggers when a branch or tag is deleted (delete). On.Deployment Triggers when a deployment is created (deployment). On.DeploymentStatus Triggers when a deployment status is provided (deployment_status). On.Discussion Triggers on discussion activity (discussion). On.DiscussionComment Triggers on discussion comment activity (discussion_comment). On.Fork Triggers when the repository is forked (fork). On.Gollum Triggers when the wiki is updated (gollum). On.ImageVersion Triggers on runner image version events (image_version). On.IssueComment Triggers on issue/pull-request comment activity (issue_comment). On.Issues Triggers on issue activity (issues). On.Label Triggers on label activity (label). On.MergeGroup Triggers when a pull request is added to a merge queue (merge_group). On.Milestone Triggers on milestone activity (milestone). On.PageBuild Triggers when someone pushes to the GitHub Pages publishing branch (page_build). On.Project Triggers on classic project activity (project). On.ProjectCard Triggers on classic project card activity (project_card). On.ProjectColumn Triggers on classic project column activity (project_column). On.Public Triggers when the repository changes from private to public (public). On.PullRequest Triggers on pull request activity (pull_request), with optional branch/tag/path filters. On.PullRequestReview Triggers on pull request review activity (pull_request_review). On.PullRequestReviewComment Triggers on pull request review comment activity (pull_request_review_comment). On.PullRequestTarget Triggers on pull request activity, running in the context of the base of the pull request (pull_request_target). Use with care — the workflow has access to repository secrets. On.Push Triggers when commits or tags are pushed (push), with optional branch/tag/path filters. All filter properties are required so that omissions are explicit; pass null for filters you do not want. On.RegistryPackage Triggers on registry package activity (registry_package). On.Release Triggers on release activity (release). On.RepositoryDispatch Triggers on a custom webhook event sent via the GitHub API (repository_dispatch). On.Schedule Triggers on a schedule (schedule). On.Status Triggers when the status of a commit changes (status). On.Watch Triggers when the repository is starred (watch). On.WorkflowCall Allows the workflow to be called from another workflow (workflow_call), making it a reusable workflow. On.WorkflowDispatch Allows the workflow to be run manually from the GitHub UI or API (workflow_dispatch). On.WorkflowRun Triggers when another workflow runs (workflow_run). On0MatchExtensions Permissions GITHUB_TOKEN permissions at workflow or job level (permissions). This is a discriminated union: use Permissions.All to grant one level to every scope (read-all / write-all), or Permissions.Exact to specify levels per scope. Permissions.All Grants the same Level to every permission scope, rendered as read-all or write-all. Permissions.Exact Grants explicit levels for individual permission scopes. Permissions0MatchExtensions PermissionsEvent Per-scope GITHUB_TOKEN permission levels. Scopes left null are omitted from the YAML and fall back to GitHub's defaults. RunsOn The runner selection for a job (runs-on). Snapshot Runner image snapshot settings for a job (snapshot), used to capture the runner state as a custom image after the job completes. Step A single step within a job. This is a discriminated union: construct a Step.UsesStep to run an action or a Step.RunStep to run shell commands. The properties on this base record are shared by both variants. Step.RunStep A step that runs shell commands (run). Step.UsesStep A step that runs a reusable action (uses), e.g. actions/checkout@v4. Provide inputs via With. Step0MatchExtensions Strategy The execution strategy for a job (strategy), defining a matrix of variations to run. WorkflowDispatchInput A typed input for the workflow_dispatch trigger. This is a discriminated union; construct WorkflowDispatchInput.String, WorkflowDispatchInput.Number, WorkflowDispatchInput.Boolean, or WorkflowDispatchInput.Choice for the desired input type. WorkflowDispatchInput.Boolean A true/false input (type: boolean). WorkflowDispatchInput.Choice A dropdown input restricted to a set of options (type: choice). WorkflowDispatchInput.Number A numeric input (type: number). WorkflowDispatchInput.String A free-text input (type: string). WorkflowDispatchInput0MatchExtensions Enums On.BranchProtectionRule.BranchProtectionType Activity types for the branch_protection_rule event. On.CheckRun.CheckRunType Activity types for the check_run event. On.CheckSuite.CheckSuiteType Activity types for the check_suite event. On.Discussion.DiscussionType Activity types for the discussion event. On.DiscussionComment.DiscussionCommentType Activity types for the discussion_comment event. On.IssueComment.IssueCommentType Activity types for the issue_comment event. On.Issues.IssuesType Activity types for the issues event. On.Label.LabelType Activity types for the label event. On.MergeGroup.MergeGroupType Activity types for the merge_group event. On.Milestone.MilestoneType Activity types for the milestone event. On.Project.ProjectType Activity types for the project event. On.ProjectCard.ProjectCardType Activity types for the project_card event. On.ProjectColumn.ProjectColumnType Activity types for the project_column event. On.PullRequest.PullRequestType Activity types for the pull_request event. On.PullRequestReview.PullRequestReviewType Activity types for the pull_request_review event. On.PullRequestReviewComment.PullRequestReviewCommentType Activity types for the pull_request_review_comment event. On.PullRequestTarget.PullRequestTargetType Activity types for the pull_request_target event. On.RegistryPackage.RegistryPackageType Activity types for the registry_package event. On.Release.ReleaseType Activity types for the release event. On.Watch.WatchType Activity types for the watch event. On.WorkflowRun.WorkflowDispatchTypes Activity types for the workflow_run event. PermissionsLevel The access level granted to a GITHUB_TOKEN permission scope.

api/Invex.StructuredText.GithubActions.G.title: Class GithubActionWriter | Invex.StructuredText
api/Invex.StructuredText.GithubActions.G.summary: Class GithubActionWriter Namespace Invex.StructuredText.GithubActions Assembly Invex.StructuredText.GithubActions.dll Serializes a GithubAction model to GitHub Actions workflow YAML. Expressions embedded in the model are rendered with GithubExpressionFormatter. [PublicAPI] public sealed class GithubActionWriter Inheritance object GithubActionWriter Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Examples var writer = new GithubActionWriter(); writer.Write(workflow); File.WriteAllText(".github/workflows/ci.yml", writer.TextWriter.ToString()); Remarks The writer accumulates output in TextWriter; call ToString() to retrieve the YAML. Use a fresh writer (or Reset(int)) for each workflow file. Properties TextWriter The underlying text writer that accumulates the generated YAML. public StructuredTextWriter TextWriter { get; init; } Property Value StructuredTextWriter Methods Write(GithubAction) Writes the complete workflow YAML for githubAction — name, run-name, triggers, permissions, env, concurrency, and all jobs with their steps. public void Write(GithubAction githubAction) Parameters githubAction GithubAction The workflow model to serialize.

api/Invex.StructuredText.GithubActions.G.title: Class GithubExpressionFormatter | Invex.StructuredText
api/Invex.StructuredText.GithubActions.G.summary: Class GithubExpressionFormatter Namespace Invex.StructuredText.GithubActions Assembly Invex.StructuredText.GithubActions.dll Renders TextExpression trees using GitHub Actions expression syntax: EvaluateExpression becomes ${{ ... }}, logic operators use && / || / !, comparisons use == / != / < / …, and functions render as contains(...), format(...), hashFiles(...), etc. Step and job references render against the steps. and needs. contexts. [PublicAPI] public sealed class GithubExpressionFormatter : TextExpressionFormatter, ITextExpressionFormatter Inheritance object TextExpressionFormatter GithubExpressionFormatter Implements ITextExpressionFormatter Inherited Members TextExpressionFormatter.Format(TextExpression) object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods Resolve(TextExpression) Rewrites a single expression node into its GitHub Actions textual form. protected override TextExpression? Resolve(TextExpression expression) Parameters expression TextExpression The node to rewrite. Returns TextExpression Exceptions ArgumentOutOfRangeException Thrown for unsupported expression types.

api/Invex.StructuredText.GithubActions.h.title: Namespace Invex.StructuredText.GithubActions | Invex.StructuredText
api/Invex.StructuredText.GithubActions.h.summary: Namespace Invex.StructuredText.GithubActions Classes DependabotConfigWriter Serializes a DependabotConfig model to Dependabot v2 configuration YAML (.github/dependabot.yml). GithubActionWriter Serializes a GithubAction model to GitHub Actions workflow YAML. Expressions embedded in the model are rendered with GithubExpressionFormatter. GithubExpressionFormatter Renders TextExpression trees using GitHub Actions expression syntax: EvaluateExpression becomes ${{ ... }}, logic operators use && / || / !, comparisons use == / != / < / …, and functions render as contains(...), format(...), hashFiles(...), etc. Step and job references render against the steps. and needs. contexts.

api/Invex.StructuredText.StructuredTextW.title: Class StructuredTextWriter | Invex.StructuredText
api/Invex.StructuredText.StructuredTextW.summary: Class StructuredTextWriter Namespace Invex.StructuredText Assembly Invex.StructuredText.dll An indentation-aware text writer for producing structured text such as YAML. Indentation is managed through disposable scopes returned by WriteSection(string?), so the nesting of the generating code mirrors the nesting of the generated output. [PublicAPI] public sealed class StructuredTextWriter Inheritance object StructuredTextWriter Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Examples var writer = new StructuredTextWriter(); using (writer.WriteSection("jobs:")) using (writer.WriteSection("build:")) writer.WriteLine("runs-on: ubuntu-latest"); var yaml = writer.ToString(); Constructors StructuredTextWriter(int) An indentation-aware text writer for producing structured text such as YAML. Indentation is managed through disposable scopes returned by WriteSection(string?), so the nesting of the generating code mirrors the nesting of the generated output. public StructuredTextWriter(int indentSize = 2) Parameters indentSize int The number of spaces written per indent level. Defaults to 2. Examples var writer = new StructuredTextWriter(); using (writer.WriteSection("jobs:")) using (writer.WriteSection("build:")) writer.WriteLine("runs-on: ubuntu-latest"); var yaml = writer.ToString(); Properties Indent The current indent level (number of nested sections), not the number of spaces. public int Indent { get; } Property Value int IndentSize The number of spaces written per indent level. public int IndentSize { get; set; } Property Value int StringBuilder The underlying buffer that accumulates the written text. public StringBuilder StringBuilder { get; init; } Property Value StringBuilder Version A counter incremented by every Reset(int). Section scopes created before a reset compare against this value and become no-ops on dispose, so stale scopes cannot corrupt the indentation of content written after the reset. public int Version { get; } Property Value int Methods Reset(int) Clears the buffer, resets the indent level to zero, and invalidates any open section scopes by incrementing Version. Use this to reuse one writer for multiple documents. public void Reset(int indentSize = 2) Parameters indentSize int The indent size to use from this point on. Defaults to 2. ToString() Returns the accumulated text. public override string ToString() Returns string Write(string?, bool) Appends text without a trailing newline. public void Write(string? text = null, bool indent = true) Parameters text string The text to append. When null, nothing is written. indent bool Whether to prefix the text with the current indentation. Pass false when continuing a line that has already been started. WriteLine(string?) Appends text as a full line at the current indent level, or a blank line when text is null. public void WriteLine(string? text = null) Parameters text string The text to append, or null to write an empty line. WriteSection(string?) Optionally writes a header line, then increases the indent level by one. public IDisposable WriteSection(string? text = null) Parameters text string An optional header line (e.g. "steps:") written at the current indent level before the indent is increased. When null or empty, only the indent changes. Returns IDisposable A scope that restores the previous indent level when disposed. Dispose it (typically via using) to close the section. Remarks If Reset(int) is called while a scope is still open, disposing that scope does nothing — the scope is tied to the Version it was created under.
